mechanics.text:
By ThE NUMBERS
ricochets that bounced off the wall over her head, but of the MechWarrior there was no sign. 'Here they come; Stephen whispered loudly in my ear; and then all thoughts of Erik Gray were forgotten_ Almost directly above , Stephen had clipped tether to the rafter and now lowered himself down into the bay at rapid pace_ He hung six meters over the floor; one hand on the thin black nylon rope he swung from and the other spraying fire from his silenced Kyosa_ rose into half-crouch, leveling the Intek Iaser rifle back along the bay and centering at the rush against Two men pitched forward as Stephen fire tore through them: chose another in the Iead and managed burst into his midsection and across his legs cutting them out from beneath him; Tnis stalled the rush, though answering fire cut the air in sharp whistling rush_ One bullet clipped my right arm, and stag gered few steps to the left and continued t0 fire  My power clip emptied again just as IOok direct hit in the chest. The force threw me back with the impact of small auto- mobile It hurt to breathe, but then the military had taught uS that pain was your friend~it let you know you weren' dead yet: clapped hand over my chest, and it came away clean_ blood. My vest had stopped the shot, though the hard twinge of pain with each breath warned of cracked rib_ Lying on my back_ staring up into the rafters of the bay; saw Stephen take two rounds The first jerked his leg back_ spinning him around_ The second grazed his arm and tore it loose from its steadying hold on the tether: He spun out of con- trol , his SMG slipping from his grip and dropping down onto the low wall of battered and abused crates give  Stephen this much_he rarely hesitates Having lost his weapon and taken disabling hit, it was no doubt mat ter of seconds before the fire directed against us swept in and hammered him   mercilessly into bleeding corpse His good hand went to his shoulder and came away with knife that flashed out  once, severing the tether ana  dropping him meters to unyielding ferrocrete_ heard bones break with sickening snaps. His right leg was bent at an impossible angle and the jagged end of bone from his lower right arm stuck through both skin and the cloth of his fatigues The   breath knocked from   him Stephen gasped in great lungfuls of air and then began swearing in at least three different languages on every pain-filled exhale_ The bay floor shook: Dumbly; for moment thought the light tremor was the aftershock of Stephen s hard fall_ Then movement caught my eye _ and barely had time to think about dodging before the OmniMech took another ungainly   step, nartowiy missing Stephen and me. stared as the Mech swiveled about and brought its left arm up_ The large bore autocannon passed right over both of us and into line with the Skye Tiger security: Fire belched outward two meters as hail of depleted uranium slugs chewed into the Atlas' lower legs and the security detail beyond _
had no doubt who was in that cockpit, though judging by the 'Mech' s jerky motions guessed he was driving without the aid neurohelmet interface_ If Erik was cutting some corners in his haste out of necessity for mismatched technology; wasn t about to com plain Another spray of autocannon fire silenced the majority of our opposition as they bled or fled the bay; not about to stand up against one of war fare'$ avatars The massive   machine swiveled about in short rocking steps facing the massive door; which Jaime now abandoned with incredible haste_ The auto cannon rounds tore into the heavy metal dooi, punching holes and in some places gashing large rents into the metal, but also smashing the upper supports_ Then the 80 ton Mech staggered forward_ shouldering Wdx through with the screech stressed and tearing metal. The door gave way from the wall and folded outward the assault machine forcing its way out into the damp Solaris night: heard lumbering crash as Erik con- tinued t0 power his way through the wall surrounding the Skye Tiger estate and then the final ground-shaking fall of 80 tons meeting earth: Between Jaime and myself, we shouldered Stephen'$ weight and carried him through the trail of debris left behind the OmniMech escape_ We saw no security in the twenty meters separating the bay from the nearest point of the wall; now crumbled pile of rubble with the meta foot 0f battlefield titan half buried in The rest of the Omni was stretched full-length over the ground on the other side of the wall; It Iay into and across Miller Street, wisps ofsteam rising where the light rain  splashed against the heated autocannon barrel, Shouts of command drift- ed out from the compound as we came Up to the OmniMech'$ head the first sounds of organized response after the past few chaotic minutes Erik was climbing out of the open hatch at the back of the armored neck: was still holding things together and the numbers don't get much more strained but we weren done yet. Resting barely conscious Stephen against the Man 0 War' $ shoulder; dug into my satchel and pulled small brick of explosives out, setting the timer for two minutes_ tossed it up t0 Erik, who grinned savagely and dropped it into the open cockpit hatch Then tossed him two larger bricks, which he stared at quizzi- cally: Slap them on the back of the left torso; told him; Right over the ammunition dump port; The sympathetic explosions of all that unspent autocannon ammo would tear the OmniMech into so much scrap_ Now we were done By the numbers Or close enough.
12
